Title: Cross-Device Clipboard Flow (OTel)
Description: View cross-device clipboard flows using W3C traceparent. Filter to root clipboard.flow spans only — each row is one complete clipboard operation (local capture or inbound sync). Click a TraceId in Seq's Traces view to see the full span tree spanning both sender and receiver devices. Post-Phase 87: cross-device flows are unified by W3C traceparent. A single TraceId covers the entire sync across peers.

Example.Description: Find all clipboard.flow root spans, then click TraceId for full cross-device trace

ExampleDescriptions: Each row is a clipboard.flow root span — one per clipboard capture or inbound sync event
For cross-device traces: sender and receiver clipboard.flow spans share the same TraceId
Click the TraceId link in Seq Traces view to see the complete sender + receiver span tree
_note: Property name casing for OTLP-ingested data in Seq 2025.2: use 'TraceId' and 'SpanName' (PascalCase). If queries return no results, try '@TraceId' or 'trace_id'. A live Seq instance is the final gate for confirming exact property names.
